intro.npc_text: What. I'm in the middle of something.

choices.text: I need access to the lab.
I'm trying to find HR. Any idea where they are?
I heard you're pretty good at FIFA.

access.npc_text: Lab access requires a cost center code. You don't have one. Raise a ticket. Three to five business weeks.

choices.text: Fine. Different question — FIFA. You play?

access_no.npc_text: No. There is no faster way. The process is the process. I built the process. Anyway, I can't think straight out here — the printer alone is running at 47 decibels, I measured it. If I could actually concentrate I might see a workaround. But I cannot. So there isn't.

hr_response.npc_text: HR? HR has never submitted a single ticket. Not one. So technically they don't exist in my system. Which means, philosophically, they might not exist at all. Ask someone who believes in them.

fifa_bait.npc_text: ...did you just bring up FIFA while I'm actively working?

fifa_ego.npc_text: Statistically dominant. Forty-three games, zero losses. xG differential of plus eleven point four. I track it on a spreadsheet. With conditional formatting. But I'm not playing right now — I have six open tickets and the noise in this building is genuinely destroying my cognition. I can literally hear the servers hum.

choices.text: What would help with the noise?

fifa_noise.npc_text: Better headphones. Right now I can hear the printer, the servers hum, someone microwaving something that smells criminal, and the receptionist sighing on a very consistent four-second cycle. I've got twelve tickets to clear and my brain is just... buffering.

fifa_refuse.npc_text: No. I don't play randoms. My record is clean and I intend to keep it that way. Raise a ticket. Three to five business weeks. Goodbye.
